title: hieracium atratum subsp. dolichaetum
intro: Hieracium atratum subsp. dolichaetum, also known as the lesser hawkweed, is a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family that is native to certain regions of Europe. The leaves and roots of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Hieracium atratum subsp. dolichaetum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves and roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
